title: Boil-Glow Rice Roll
description: A kid-friendly Thai dish featuring tender rice paper sheets boiled until slightly chewy and served with a hint of bitterness, perfect for a light meal.
instructions: Pour vegetable oil in a shallow pan until it reaches about half the height of your rice paper sheet.
Place a rice paper sheet on a clean surface and lightly drizzle with a few drops of vegetable oil to prevent sticking.
Add minced shallots, finely chopped garlic, and fish sauce (if using) evenly spread over one side of the rice paper.
Sprinkle minced shallots and basil leaves on top of the minced garlic mixture.
Fold in half along the short edge first, then fold both ends toward each other to seal the roll.
Carefully place the sealed rice rolls into the oil-soaked pan. Cook for about 30 seconds to a minute or until the sides begin to puff and turn golden brown.
Gently lift out with a pair of tongs and sprinkle sesame seeds over top before serving warm.
